signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Risk + Carry + Vol
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.32 — price holds above the 160.00 psychological handle, a level previously associated with BoJ verbal and physical intervention. Despite the breach, 300bp Fed-BoJ carry (Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75%) sustains the bid while VIX at 16.1 (normal volatility) limits safe-haven JPY demand. Options market is pricing significant tail risk with a -1.20 25d RR put skew and a +3.8% VRP (IV 7.2% vs HV30 3.4%), signaling that institutions are paying a heavy premium for downside protection against a sudden BoJ-induced reversal. Sustained trading above 160.00 increases the probability of immediate MoF action.

signals.title: USD/CHF — SNB Yield Shock + Carry
signals.text: SNB sight deposit rates confirmed at 0.00% (up to threshold) and -0.25% (above threshold) as of June 16, while 10Y Swiss bond yields retreat to 0.393%. USD/CHF at 0.7947 remains supported by a massive +375bp carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs SNB 0.0%). The real rate differential (+1.31% USD vs -0.31% CHF) further favors USD, though the pair's 1M gain of +1.34% faces technical resistance as Swiss yields stabilize. Watch the SNB meeting on June 18 for any shift in the sight deposit tiering mechanism.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs Retail Divergence
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5833 exhibits a sharp positioning divergence: COT LF net short -22,354 (specs bearish) vs retail 86% long (extreme retail bullish). This contrarian setup is reinforced by a negative CESI (net -4 misses), suggesting fundamental data is failing to support the retail long thesis. With RBNZ at 2.25% vs Fed 3.75% creating a -150bp carry deficit for the Kiwi, the retail long position is highly vulnerable to a capitulation squeeze if price breaks the recent lows. Watch for RBNZ hike signaling on July 8 to potentially rescue the retail position; otherwise, the path of least resistance remains lower.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Volatility Premium + Positioning
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3423 shows the highest volatility risk premium in the G10 space at +5.0% (IV 11.0% vs HV30 6.0%). This suggests the options market is pricing in significant event risk for the BoE meeting on June 18, despite a neutral 0bp carry spread (Fed 3.75% vs BoE 3.75%). COT LF net long +21,961 (61.7% long) indicates specs are moderately positioned for GBP outperformance, but the high VRP warns that the cost of hedging is currently prohibitive for new trend followers. Watch for a vol crush post-BoE to signal a cleaner entry.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Retail Extreme
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.3999 is trading at a significant dislocation from energy prices, with the USD/CAD vs WTI correlation falling to -0.284 (z = -1.61σ). While retail is 92% short (extreme contrarian signal), the +150bp carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs BoC 2.25%) and positive USD CESI (+22) support the current USD strength. The breakdown in the oil-link suggests USD/CAD is currently functioning as a pure interest-rate and data-divergence play rather than a commodity proxy. Watch for a return to the 1.4000 level to trigger retail stops.
